title: Structure of Chloroxylon Swietenia Gum. Part I. Nature of Sugars  Present and the Structure of Aldobiouronic Acid
author: S. Bose
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: \(Chloroxylon\)   \(awictienia\)  gum on hydrolysis yields a mixture of D-galactose, L-arabinose, D-galacturonio acid, 4-O-rnethyl-D-glueuronic acid, and three other minor sugars. Graded hydrolysis of the gum provides an ahloobionronic acid, which has been shown to be 6-O-( \(\alpha\) -D-galactopyranosyluronic acid)-D-galactose from methylation studies.
steps_raw: \(Chloroxylon\)   \(awictienia\)  gum on hydrolysis yields a mixture of D-galactose, L-arabinose, D-galacturonio acid, 4-O-rnethyl-D-glueuronic acid, and three other minor sugars. Graded hydrolysis of the gum provides an ahloobionronic acid, which has been shown to be 6-O-( \(\alpha\) -D-galactopyranosyluronic acid)-D-galactose from methylation studies.
